title: Initial CySA+ Practice Set
summary: 20 CySA+ practice questions and 5 SOC investigation scenarios focused on authentication, phishing, persistence, DNS tunneling, lateral movement, and vulnerability validation.

questions.question:
A SIEM alert shows:
- Multiple failed logins
- One successful login
- Event ID 4672 shortly afterward

The account used is a privileged service account.

Which of the following is the MOST likely concern?
questions.myAnswer: B, because multiple failed logins followed by 4672 are highly suspicious.

questions.question:
An analyst detects the following process chain:

OUTLOOK.EXE
 └── WINWORD.EXE
      └── powershell.exe -nop -w hidden -enc ...

Which of the following is the MOST likely initial attack vector?
questions.myAnswer: B, because this behavior is likely caused by malicious file.

questions.question:
A scanner reports a critical Apache vulnerability on a public web server. The server is behind a reverse proxy and the backend patch level is unknown.

What should the analyst do FIRST?
questions.myAnswer: C, because server is behind reverse proxy, so report showing critical Apache vulnerability is probably false positive, because of false banner grabbing.

questions.question:
A host repeatedly contacts a newly registered domain every 60 seconds over HTTPS using small amounts of traffic.

What is the MOST likely explanation?
questions.myAnswer: C, because newly registered domains and timed requests are usual for C2.

questions.question: Which of the following BEST represents credential stuffing?
options: One password attempted against many users
questions.myAnswer: B, because in this case attacker gets access to pairs of stolen creds, and uses them on various accounts.

questions.question: Which framework is MOST useful for comparing adversary TTPs?
questions.myAnswer: C, because MITRE ATT&CK is a framework that include various APT TTPs.

questions.question:
An analyst suspects DNS tunneling.

Which of the following indicators BEST supports this suspicion?
questions.myAnswer: A, because long queries are used to obfuscate/mask data exfil.

questions.question:
A Linux host shows:

Accepted password for devuser
sudo: devuser : COMMAND=/bin/bash
sudo: devuser : COMMAND=/usr/bin/nmap

What is MOST likely occurring?
questions.myAnswer: B, because /bin/bash indicates priv esc and nmap is tool used for reconnaissance.

questions.question: Which of the following BEST reduces SIEM false positives?
questions.myAnswer: C, because tuning and narrowing detection rules would create less false positives.

questions.question:
A workstation has confirmed malware execution.

What should be performed FIRST?
questions.myAnswer: C, because first step in IR is containment

questions.question: Which of the following is a persistence mechanism?
questions.myAnswer: A, because ICMP is used for persistence? (not confident, forgot what ICMP means)

questions.question:
An analyst observes:

WINWORD.EXE → powershell.exe → external IP

Which phase of the attack is MOST likely occurring?
questions.myAnswer: A, because connecting to external IP indicates attempt to establish persistence.

questions.question:
A company wants to detect impossible travel logins.

Which solution BEST fits this requirement?
questions.myAnswer: B, because UEBA is used to detect behavior anomalies, and impossible travel login is a suspicious anomaly

questions.question: Which of the following is MOST likely a false positive vulnerability finding?
questions.myAnswer: C, because false banner grabbing is a common false positive indicator, like with server behind reverse proxy

questions.question:
An analyst notices encoded PowerShell commands using:
powershell.exe -enc

What does this MOST likely indicate?
questions.myAnswer: B, because -enc means encoded/obfuscated payload execution

questions.question: Which phase of incident response includes removing malware persistence artifacts?
questions.myAnswer: C, because eradication means removing any possible threat before doing post-incident analysis or recovering system

questions.question:
A user downloads thousands of sensitive files at 3:00 AM and triggers a DLP alert.

What should the analyst do FIRST?
options: Verify the activity and investigate the session
questions.myAnswer: B, because this behavior is suspicious (especially because of DLP alert and time), but not enough to immediately isolate the workstation.

questions.question: Which log source is MOST useful for detecting WMI lateral movement?
questions.myAnswer: C, because WMI is Windows framework

questions.question:
An attacker tries one password against hundreds of accounts.

Which attack is this?
questions.myAnswer: B, because in password spraying technique attacker uses one known password for many different accounts.

questions.question:
A compromised host:
- maintains persistence
- reconnects externally after reboot
- creates registry Run keys

What severity should MOST likely be assigned?
questions.myAnswer: D, because this is highly risky and dangerous situation, especially because of attackers ability to maintain persistence.

scenarios.title: Phishing → PowerShell → Persistence
tasks: Identify the attack chain.
What is the MOST likely initial vector?
What persistence mechanism is present?
What should the analyst do FIRST?
What additional logs would help most?
Write a simple detection logic idea.

myAnswers.answer: Phishing -> malicious file -> powershell execution -> connection to external ip -> persistence
Malicious file execution by opening phishing emails attachment
Connection to external ip and registry task creation

scenarios.title: DNS Tunneling & Data Exfiltration
tasks: What is MOST likely occurring?
Why are the DNS queries suspicious?
What should the analyst investigate FIRST?
What containment action is MOST appropriate?
What SIEM detection logic could detect this?

myAnswers.answer: Possible DNS tunneling detection, high entropy suspicious domains

scenarios.title: Credential Attack & Possible Lateral Movement
tasks: What attack behavior is MOST likely occurring?
Which MITRE ATT&CK techniques are involved?
What should be prioritized FIRST?
What systems/accounts should be investigated?
What evidence would confirm lateral movement?

myAnswers.answer: T1041 (Exfiltration Over C2 Channel) and T1110.003 (Brute Force: Password Spraying)

scenarios.title: Potential Apache False Positive
tasks: Is this automatically a critical emergency?
What should be validated FIRST?
Why could this be a false positive?
How should the vulnerability be prioritized?
What compensating controls already exist?

myAnswers.answer: No, because of highly possible false banner grabbing
Backend patch level, and then vulnerability risk level
Because server is behind reverse proxy, so scanner is showing misleading info

scenarios.title: Multi-Alert Incident Correlation

sections.title: Alert B
items: Repeated HTTPS traffic to newly registered domain every 60 seconds

sections.title: Alert C
items: User reports system slowdown after opening invoice attachment

sections.title: Alert D
items: Vulnerability scanner reports outdated Apache version behind reverse proxy

tasks: Rank alerts from MOST urgent to LEAST urgent.
Which alerts are likely related?
Which alert may be false positive/noise?
What should be contained FIRST?
What evidence strongly suggests active compromise?

myAnswers.answer: A and E, C and A, A and B, B and E
Obfuscated powershell command from Word, repeated traffic to new domains, weird DNS queries
